speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell said the FOMC left rates unchanged, described current policy as appropriate, and emphasized a data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting approach while reiterating commitment to 2% inflation and maximum employment.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell described policy as around the high end of neutral or mildly restrictive, said the labor market looks stable, and stressed that inflation remains the main concern.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell highlighted progress on inflation and labor market conditions while signaling that policy remains focused on returning inflation sustainably to target.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell announced a 25 bp rate cut and framed the move as balancing risks to employment and inflation.

speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell reiterated that the Fed remains in a good position to respond in either direction, with no one calling for a hike and future decisions dependent on incoming data.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.62%, unemployment at 4.3%.
